body:
MORNING INTEL — 2026-07-13 (snapshot 2026-07-13_7)
Regime data: VIX 17.33 | HY OAS 269 | Breadth 63.6%
AMZN: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
NVDA: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
SPY: TRADE | BULLISH conf 53.333333333333336% | Range 729.07–768.99 | put_credit_spread x1
Portfolio risk after fills: pending Artha confirmation
Discipline: 30d system PnL $0 | override PnL $0
Data quality: all sources OK

structured.takeaway: The current regime shows elevated volatility with VIX at 17.33, a steep contango between 9D (15.31) and 3M (19.79), and HY OAS at 269 bps, signaling cautious risk appetite amid rising US-Iran tensions that are dragging Nasdaq leaders and SPY lower, with SPY at $749.03 and a negative sentiment of -1. NVDA at $204.09 and AMZN at $247.35 both carry strong positive sentiment (+1) supported by a thematic generational transfer favoring NVIDIA and Micron, while Amazon trades near decade lows, presenting a compelling value entry. The key near-term catalyst is the escalation of geopolitical risk from Iran strikes, which could exacerbate market volatility and overshadow the inflation narrative that hints at a potential Fed hike, as flagged by Waller, keeping yields elevated (US10Y 4.54%). Watch bank earnings next week closely, especially Citigroup, as credit stress could amplify if HY spreads widen further. Trade signal gates remain fully active, favoring selective long exposure in NVDA and AMZN while maintaining defensive posture on broad indices.

SPY.sentiment_driver: Nasdaq leading stocks lower as U.S.-Iran tensions escalate

NVDA.sentiment_driver: NVIDIA and Micron the Winners in generational transfer
NVDA.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

AMZN.sentiment_driver: Amazon stock hasn't been this cheap in over a decade
AMZN.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

news.title: Nasdaq leading stocks lower in final hour as U.S.-Iran tensions escalate — live
Inflation to fall for first time in 6 years. Consumers won't see much relief.
A Generational Transfer Is Happening: Amazon, Google, Microsoft Are the Losers. NVIDIA and Micron the Winners.
Amazon Stock Hasn't Been This Cheap in Over a Decade. Has the Sell-Off Gone Too Far?
A hot inflation reading this week could mean a rate hike soon, Fed’s Waller says
Nasdaq and S&P 500 open lower as Iran launches new strikes
Why Citigroup is the one to watch when banks report earnings this week
Economic calendar: Treasury balance today; CPI and PPI this week
India's inflation accelerates to 4.38% in June, exceeding forecasts
The stock-market rally now hinges more on AI than oil
Oil prices rise, stock futures dip after latest flare-up of U.S.-Iran strikes

narratives.this_week: Bank earnings dominate this week with JPM, BAC, WFC, GS, and MS reporting results between July 14-15. Investors will focus on loan growth, credit quality, and trading revenue to gauge financial sector resilience amid a VIX at 17.33 and HY OAS steady at 269 bps. Positive surprises here could buoy SPY by alleviating recession fears, while any weakness might pressure financials and weigh on broader market sentiment.
narratives.next_week: Next week features key tech and industrial earnings, notably GOOGL, TSLA, and HON, alongside real estate names like CCI and DLR. Market participants should watch for guidance on consumer demand and supply chain dynamics, especially from GOOGL and TSLA, which have outsized influence on SPY and NVDA’s sector peers. Strong results could reinforce tech leadership and support AMZN, while softness might signal broader cyclical headwinds.
narratives.next_month: In the following month, the calendar intensifies with mega-cap tech earnings from MSFT, AAPL, and AMZN clustered around July 29-30, alongside the FOMC meeting and July PCE data. These events will be critical for assessing growth and inflation trajectories, directly impacting SPY’s direction and tech sector multiples including NVDA. Energy and materials earnings late in the month will also provide insight into commodity-driven inflation pressures, influencing cross-asset flows into cyclicals and defensives.
